title: Health and safety managers and officers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate entertainment and recreational activities and operations of a recreational facility, including cruise ships and parks. All personal service managers not listed separately. Manage or coordinate fitness and wellness programs and services. Manage and train staff of wellness specialists, health educators, or fitness instructors. (Based on 58 related US occupations.)

work_activities.element_name: Communicating with Supervisors, Peers, or Subordinates
Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards
Interpreting the Meaning of Information for Others
Communicating with People Outside the Organization
Judging the Qualities of Objects, Services, or People
Estimating the Quantifiable Characteristics of Products, Events, or Information
Providing Consultation and Advice to Others
Coordinating the Work and Activities of Others
Resolving Conflicts and Negotiating with Others
Drafting, Laying Out, and Specifying Technical Devices, Parts, and Equipment
Operating Vehicles, Mechanized Devices, or Equipment
Performing for or Working Directly with the Public

work_context.element_name: Face-to-Face Discussions with Individuals and Within Teams
Work With or Contribute to a Work Group or Team
Deal With External Customers or the Public in General
Coordinate or Lead Others in Accomplishing Work Activities
Health and Safety of Other Workers
Work Outcomes and Results of Other Workers
Dealing With Unpleasant, Angry, or Discourteous People
Dealing with Violent or Physically Aggressive People
Outdoors, Exposed to All Weather Conditions
In an Open Vehicle or Operating Equipment
In an Enclosed Vehicle or Operate Enclosed Equipment
Exposed to Sounds, Noise Levels that are Distracting or Uncomfortable
Exposed to Very Hot or Cold Temperatures
Exposed to Extremely Bright or Inadequate Lighting Conditions
Exposed to Cramped Work Space, Awkward Positions
Exposed to Minor Burns, Cuts, Bites, or Stings
Spend Time Climbing Ladders, Scaffolds, or Poles
Spend Time Kneeling, Crouching, Stooping, or Crawling
Spend Time Keeping or Regaining Balance
Spend Time Using Your Hands to Handle, Control, or Feel Objects, Tools, or Controls
Spend Time Bending or Twisting Your Body
Wear Common Protective or Safety Equipment such as Safety Shoes, Glasses, Gloves, Hearing Protection, Hard Hats, or Life Jackets
Wear Specialized Protective or Safety Equipment such as Breathing Apparatus, Safety Harness, Full Protection Suits, or Radiation Protection
Impact of Decisions on Co-workers or Company Results
Importance of Being Exact or Accurate
Pace Determined by Speed of Equipment

tasks.task: Develop, prepare, and coordinate grant applications and grant-related activities to obtain funding for health education programs and related work.
Maintain inventories of hazardous materials or hazardous wastes and ensure that they are handled properly.
Conduct audits and participate in investigations at hazardous waste or industrial sites, including the collection and analysis of samples of hazardous materials.
Inspect specified areas to ensure the presence of safety equipment and supplies, and conduct health-related facility inspections to comply with public health legislation and regulations.
Maintain or update emergency response plans, and conduct health needs assessments and public health surveys tailored to community needs.
Investigate accidents and health-related complaints, recommend protective measures for workers, and verify the proper use of safety equipment.
Provide health and safety orientations for new employees and conduct safety training or educational programs on health protection practices and equipment use.
Collaborate with engineers, health specialists, and civic groups to implement control measures for hazardous conditions and assess community health resources.
Document and analyze data related to workplace incidents to identify trends in health hazards, illnesses, or accidents, and prepare reports on findings.
Inspect workplaces for compliance with environmental health and safety standards, and recommend corrective actions for violations found.
Educate the public about health issues and enforce health legislation through presentations and community programs.
Develop and maintain health education libraries and prepare informational materials addressing public health concerns.
Prepare or review specifications for safety equipment purchases to ensure compliance with health and safety standards.
Conduct investigations and inspections related to safety standards, document violations, and recommend corrective actions in response to complaints.
Collect and analyze environmental samples related to health hazards, ensuring compliance with environmental permits.
Maintain records of activities and prepare documents necessary for legal proceedings or audits related to public health.
Conduct training for workers on safety procedures applicable to their roles.
Plan and conduct emergency response drills to prepare for health and safety contingencies.
Collect data regarding potential hazards from new equipment related to health and safety practices.
Evaluate situations involving health and safety to ensure procedural compliance and worker well-being.
Collaborate with various stakeholders to develop and implement public health measures and safety standards.

Inspect camp sites, forest tracts, and logging areas to ensure compliance with regulations and identify hazards such as fire risks or contamination, and recommend appropriate prevention or decontamination measures.
Relay messages about emergencies, accidents, locations of crew and personnel, and fire hazard conditions.
Analyze samples such as air, water, or solids for contaminants and evaluate test results against specifications to ensure safety and compliance.
Collect evidence or samples, including air, water, and materials, and prepare documentation relevant to safety, contamination tests, or hazardous materials clean-up initiatives.

Monitor and evaluate the effectiveness of safety procedures, including radiation exposure monitoring, equipment maintenance, and emergency response protocols, documenting any anomalies or issues found.
Prepare detailed reports and documentation, including environmental assessments and analytical methods, to support safety compliance and operational efficiency.
Participate in internal assessments, audits, and collaborate with relevant departments to ensure safety and compliance standards are met across operations and activities.
Train staff in safety procedures and ensure that protocol adherence is maintained among all personnel, providing technical support when needed.
Investigate and troubleshoot equipment issues, ensuring that all operations maintain compliance with safety regulations and best practices.
Conduct statistical analysis where applicable to support safety and quality assurance initiatives, addressing pollution data and developing prevention programs.

Monitor testing procedures to ensure all tests are performed according to established specifications and protocols; participate in out-of-specification investigations and recommend corrective actions; and perform validations or transfers of analytical methods according to applicable policies or guidelines.

Prepare or review necessary method transfer documentation including protocols or reports; write or revise standard quality control operating procedures; and maintain and apply knowledge of current policies, regulations, and industrial processes.

Review data from laboratories to ensure accuracy and compliance; and conduct or coordinate testing of air quality, noise, temperature, or radiation levels to verify compliance with health and safety regulations.
Receive and inspect raw materials; conduct routine and non-routine analyses of materials or samples; and compile laboratory test data and perform appropriate analyses.
Ensure laboratory cleanliness and safety standards are maintained; and write and revise safety regulations and codes.

Inspect food products and processing procedures to determine whether products are safe to eat; monitor operations and sanitary conditions in processing plants; and take emergency actions if product safety is compromised.
Identify and recommend energy savings strategies to achieve energy-efficient operations; train personnel on safety laws and regulations; and prepare energy-related documentation and reports.
Verify integrity and accuracy of data in analysis systems; and write reports of findings and recommendations, advising clients on necessary corrective actions.
Evaluate adequacy of actions taken to correct health inspection violations; and conduct environmental studies on health and safety regulations compliance.
Compile, analyze, and interpret statistical data related to occupational illnesses and accidents; and participate in workplace incident investigations to obtain information about work environments.
Monitor and analyze energy consumption; and review plans and specifications for new machinery or equipment to ensure safety compliance.
Provide consultative services related to equipment or product evaluation, training staff in safety measures, and design or direct safety inspections of facilities and procedures.
Conduct industrial hygiene research; evaluate potential health hazards from product misuse; and develop industry standards for product safety.
Assess health risks through consultation with medical professionals; and collect samples for testing compliance with safety and health regulations.
Review employee safety programs; and provide expert testimony in litigation related to health and safety issues.
Train personnel on safety equipment use; and conduct or coordinate worker training in safety laws and regulations compliance.
Manage the implementation of energy management projects to ensure compliance with regulations and standards.
Evaluate product designs for safety and recommend procedures for detection, prevention, and elimination of hazards.
Investigate industrial accidents, injuries, or occupational diseases to determine causes and preventive measures; examine accidents to obtain data for use in design of preventive measures.

Conduct research to evaluate safety levels for products; conduct tests of new nanotechnology products, processes, or systems; and develop training materials and conduct training sessions on fire protection.

Design fire detection equipment, alarm systems, and fire extinguishing devices and systems; advise architects, builders, and other construction personnel on fire prevention equipment and techniques and on fire code and standard interpretation and compliance; and review building plans to verify compliance with fire code.

Monitor nuclear facility operations to identify any design, construction, or operation practices that violate safety regulations and laws or could jeopardize safe operations; and evaluate fire department performance and the laws and regulations affecting fire prevention or fire safety.

Recommend preventive measures to be taken in the handling of nuclear technology, based on data obtained from operations monitoring or from evaluation of test results; and design or develop nuclear equipment, such as reactor cores, radiation shielding, or associated instrumentation or control mechanisms.

Develop or contribute to the development of plans to remediate or restore environments affected by nuclear radiation, such as waste disposal sites; and investigate mechanical failures or unexpected maintenance problems.
Conduct training or in-services to educate clinicians and other personnel on proper use of equipment; and advise hospital administrators on the planning, acquisition, and use of medical equipment.
Attend workshops, seminars, or conferences to present or obtain information regarding fire prevention and protection.

Study the relationships between ignition sources and materials to determine how fires start; develop plans for the prevention of destruction by fire, wind, and water; identify corrective actions necessary to bring properties into compliance with applicable fire codes, laws, regulations, and standards, and explain these measures to property owners or their representatives.

Conduct fire exit drills to monitor and evaluate evacuation procedures; develop or review fire exit plans; teach public education programs on fire safety and prevention; write detailed reports of fire inspections performed, fire code violations observed, and corrective recommendations offered.
Consult with authorities to discuss safety regulations and to recommend changes as necessary; conduct inspections using survey instruments, metering devices, tape measures, or test equipment.
Inspect and monitor construction sites to ensure adherence to safety standards, building codes, or specifications; prepare reports and document project activities and data.

Inspect bridges, dams, highways, buildings, wiring, plumbing, electrical circuits, sewers, heating systems, or foundations during and after construction for structural quality, general safety, or conformance to specifications and codes.

Evaluate premises for cleanliness, such as proper garbage disposal or lack of vermin infestation; inspect facilities or installations to determine their environmental impact; conduct environmental hazard inspections to identify or quantify problems such as asbestos, poor air quality, or water contamination; monitor construction activities to ensure that environmental regulations are not violated.
Conduct training sessions on new material products, applications, or manufacturing methods for customers and their employees.

Inspect properties that store, handle, and use hazardous materials to ensure compliance with laws, codes, and regulations, and issue hazardous materials permits to facilities found in compliance. Conduct inspections and acceptance testing of fire protection systems to verify compliance with standards. Inspect buildings to locate hazardous conditions and fire code violations. Inspect sites to assess environmental damage or monitor cleanup progress. Supervise and participate in the inspection of properties to ensure compliance with applicable fire codes, ordinances, laws, regulations, and standards.

Analyze evidence and other information to determine the probable cause of fire or explosion. Photograph damage and evidence related to causes of fires or explosions to document investigation findings. Examine fire sites and collect evidence to determine the cause of a fire. Prepare and maintain reports of investigation results and records of convicted arsonists. Testify in court cases involving fires, suspected arson, and false alarms. Recommend changes to fire prevention procedures and evaluate fire station procedures for efficiency and enforcement.

Analyze and manage fire prevention programs, supervise staff, and coordinate training for firefighters. Direct firefighters in assigned duties, including firefighting, medical care, and hazardous materials response. Develop and coordinate wellness programs, including training in first aid or CPR.

Coordinate efforts with other organizations, such as law enforcement agencies, to enhance fire safety measures and ensure compliance. Communicate fire details to superiors and interagency dispatch centers, and manage relationships with relevant regulatory agencies.

Conduct environmental audits to ensure adherence to environmental standards, review or modify policies to comply with environmental regulations, and develop compliance or reporting tools. Design or implement plans for environmental remediation and monitor remediation projects to ensure compliance with regulations.

Plan or implement brownfield redevelopment projects for safety and compliance, prepare application reports for environmental projects, and maintain records of actions related to environmental redevelopment projects. Conduct feasibility or cost-benefit studies for environmental remediation projects and prepare project status reports.

Attend training to maintain current knowledge of fire prevention, safety, and firefighting procedures. Study and interpret fire safety codes for issuing hazardous materials permits. Maintain knowledge of applicable fire laws and standards to ensure compliance in training and operations.
Assist engineers in the development of new safety protocols and environmental strategies, utilizing computer skills and managing websites for program communication.
Prepare documentation for environmental projects, including wind project documentation, and coordinate development activities to meet project needs.
Monitor inventory levels and order supplies for safety and environmental compliance. Evaluate and recommend equipment modifications, manage costs, and negotiate contracts for remediation services.
Provide training on hazardous material or waste management procedures and technologies to ensure compliance with safety standards.

Participate in continuing education classes to maintain current knowledge of industry. Maintain client databases. Direct facility maintenance or repair. Develop or implement marketing strategies. Verify staff credentials, such as educational and certification requirements. Schedule staff or supervise scheduling. Respond to customer inquiries or complaints. Recruit, interview, or hire employees. Perform accounting duties, such as recording daily cash flow, preparing bank deposits, or generating financial statements. Monitor operations to ensure compliance with applicable health, safety, or hygiene standards. Inventory products and order new supplies. Establish spa budgets and financial goals. Develop staff service or retail goals and guide staff in goal achievement. Coordinate facility schedules to maximize usage and efficiency. Check spa equipment to ensure proper functioning. Assess employee performance and suggest ways to improve work. Train staff in the use or sale of products, programs, or activities. Plan or direct spa services and programs. Inform staff of job responsibilities, performance expectations, client service standards, or corporate policies and guidelines. Identify and apply for project funding. Evaluate fitness and wellness programs to determine their effectiveness. Schedule guest appointments. Sell products, services, or memberships. Direct the preparation and submission of regulatory agency applications, reports, or correspondence. Provide responses to regulatory agencies regarding product information or issues. Establish procedures or systems for publishing document submissions in hardcopy or electronic formats. Develop and maintain standard operating procedures or local working practices. Train staff in regulatory policies or procedures. Review all regulatory agency submission materials to ensure timeliness, accuracy, comprehensiveness, or compliance with regulatory standards. Monitor emerging trends regarding industry regulations to determine potential impacts on organizational processes. Maintain current knowledge of relevant regulations, including proposed and final rules. Implement or monitor complaint processing systems to ensure effective and timely resolution of all complaint investigations. Develop regulatory strategies and implementation plans for the preparation and submission of new products. Communicate regulatory information to multiple departments and ensure that information is interpreted correctly. Provide regulatory guidance to departments or development project teams regarding design, development, evaluation, or marketing of products. Lead or support negotiations involving tax agreements or abatements, power purchase agreements, land use, or interconnection agreements. Prepare or implement budgets and strategic, operational, purchasing, or maintenance plans. Maintain documentation of compliance activities, such as complaints received or investigation outcomes. Serve as a confidential point of contact for employees to communicate with management, seek clarification on issues or dilemmas, or report irregularities. Develop or coordinate fitness and wellness programs or services. Supervise fitness or wellness specialists, such as fitness instructors, nutritionists, or health educators. Develop fitness or wellness classes, such as yoga, aerobics, strength training, or aquatics, ensuring a diversity of class offerings. Conduct or facilitate training sessions or seminars for wellness and fitness staff. Maintain wellness- and fitness-related schedules, records, or reports. Manage or oversee fitness or recreation facilities, ensuring safe and clean facilities and equipment. Recommend or approve new program or service offerings to promote wellness and fitness, produce revenues, or minimize costs. Advise internal management or business partners on the implementation or operation of compliance programs. Respond to customer, public, or media requests for information about wellness programs or services. Conduct needs assessments or surveys to determine interest in, or satisfaction with, wellness and fitness programs, events, or services. Develop marketing campaigns to promote a healthy lifestyle or participation in fitness or wellness programs. Maintain or arrange for maintenance of fitness equipment or facilities. Provide individual support or counseling in general wellness or nutrition. Track attendance, participation, or performance data related to wellness events. Plan programs of events or schedules of activities. Write and present strategies for recreational facility programming using customer or employee data. Train workers in company procedures or policy. Talk to customers to convey information about events or activities. Track and maintain records for wind operations, such as site performance, downtime events, parts usage, or substation events. Maintain operations records, such as work orders, site inspection forms, or other documentation. Review, negotiate, or approve wind farm contracts. Develop relationships and communicate with customers, site managers, developers, land owners, authorities, utility representatives, or residents. Prepare wind field operational budgets. Supervise employees or subcontractors to ensure quality of work or adherence to safety regulations or policies. Manage activities such as audits, regulatory agency inspections, or product recalls. Maintain current knowledge of industry changes, trends, or best practices

Train staff in regulatory policies or procedures. Evaluate new software publishing systems and confer with regulatory agencies concerning news or updates on electronic publishing of submissions. Review materials such as marketing literature or user manuals to ensure that regulatory agency requirements are met. Monitor compliance systems to ensure their effectiveness. Report violations of compliance or regulatory standards to duly authorized enforcement agencies as appropriate or required. Discuss emerging compliance issues to ensure that management and employees are informed about compliance reporting systems, policies, and practices. Conduct periodic internal reviews or audits to ensure that compliance procedures are followed. Identify compliance issues that require follow-up or investigation. Provide assistance to internal or external auditors in compliance reviews. Provide employee training on compliance related topics, policies, or procedures. Manage warranty repair or replacement services.

Clean equipment and areas of amusement park, cruise ship or other recreational facilities. Operate, drive, or explain the use of mechanical equipment in amusement parks, cruise ships, or other recreational facilities.

Assign tasks and work hours to staff. Verify that software technology is in place to adequately provide oversight and monitoring in all required areas. Supervise surveillance, detection, or criminal processing related to theft and criminal cases. Confirm that all regulatory policies and procedures have been documented, implemented, and communicated. Interview and hire associates to fill staff vacancies. Verify correct use and maintenance of physical security systems, such as closed-circuit television, merchandise tags, and burglar alarms.
Discuss compliance and risk management strategies as they relate to wellness and fitness programs or services.

Administer first aid in emergency situations, enforce safety rules and regulations, and inspect or test materials, stock, vehicles, equipment, or facilities to ensure that they are safe, free of defects, and consistent with specifications.

Coordinate or conduct internal investigations of problems such as employee theft and violations of corporate loss prevention policies, and develop and maintain partnerships with federal, state, or local law enforcement agencies or members of the retail loss prevention community.
Dispatch personnel and vehicles in response to telephone or radio reports of emergencies, and monitor field work to ensure proper performance and use of materials.
Maintain or verify records of time, materials, expenditures, or crew activities, and prepare, compile, and submit reports on work activities, operations, production, or work-related accidents.
Requisition needed personnel, supplies, equipment, parts, or repair services, and compute or estimate cash, payroll, transportation, personnel, or storage requirements.

Recommend or implement personnel actions, such as employee selection, evaluation, rewards, or disciplinary actions, and resolve worker problems or collaborate with employees to assist in problem resolution.

Plan work assignments and equipment allocations to meet transportation, operations, or production goals, and interpret transportation or tariff regulations, shipping orders, safety regulations, or company policies and procedures for workers.

Confer with customers, supervisors, contractors, or other personnel to exchange information or to resolve problems, and explain and demonstrate work tasks to new workers or assign training tasks to experienced workers.
Drive vehicles or operate machines or equipment to complete work assignments or to assist workers, and inspect equipment, such as rides, games, and vehicles, to detect wear and damage.
Review orders, production schedules, blueprints, or shipping or receiving notices to determine work sequences and material shipping dates, types, volumes, or destinations.
